project: RDY Masterclass App — Feature Completion Sprint
description: Complete missing features for the RDY Masterclass mentoring platform. Next.js 14 + tRPC + Drizzle ORM + PostgreSQL. DB runs in Docker (rdy-postgres, port 5435). PM2 process manager. Apply migrations via: cat drizzle/XXXX.sql | docker exec -i rdy-postgres psql -U rdy -d rdy. After code changes: npx next build && pm2 restart rdy. Design system: warm beige (#F0EAE0), orange accent (#FF8C42), use bg-background not bg-white.

userStories.title: Remove duplicate Program Builder page
userStories.description: The app has both /admin/curriculum-builder and /admin/program-builder which are near-identical. Remove /admin/curriculum-builder entirely (delete the directory src/app/admin/curriculum-builder/). Update the sidebar in src/components/app-sidebar.tsx to only show 'RDY Program' pointing to /admin/program-builder. Remove any imports or references to curriculum-builder in other files.
acceptance: src/app/admin/curriculum-builder/ directory no longer exists
Sidebar shows only 'RDY Program' link to /admin/program-builder
No broken imports or references remain
npx next build succeeds

userStories.title: Redirect old diary and patterns pages to Reflect
userStories.description: The old standalone pages /mentee/diary and /mentee/patterns are replaced by the combined Reflect page at /mentee/calendar/tracking. Replace the content of src/app/mentee/diary/page.tsx with a client-side redirect to /mentee/calendar/tracking. Do the same for src/app/mentee/patterns/page.tsx. Use next/navigation redirect.
acceptance: Visiting /mentee/diary redirects to /mentee/calendar/tracking
Visiting /mentee/patterns redirects to /mentee/calendar/tracking
No dead links in the app
npx next build succeeds

userStories.title: Class auto-numbering (YYNN format)
userStories.description: When creating a new class in /admin/classes, auto-generate a class number in YYNN format (e.g., 2601, 2602). YY = last 2 digits of current year, NN = sequential number starting from 01. Add a 'classNumber' varchar field to the classes table in src/lib/db/schema.ts. In the create class tRPC mutation (src/lib/trpc/routers/classes.ts), auto-calculate the next number by querying existing classes for the current year. Display the class number in the classes management list. Create a migration SQL file.
acceptance: classes table has class_number column
Creating a new class auto-assigns the next YYNN number
Class number is displayed in admin class list
Migration SQL file exists in drizzle/ directory
Migration applied: cat drizzle/XXXX.sql | docker exec -i rdy-postgres psql -U rdy -d rdy
npx next build succeeds

userStories.title: Mentor UI for setting tracking categories per mentee
userStories.description: Add a UI in the mentor's view where the mentor can configure tracking categories for a specific mentee. The tRPC procedure mentor.setMenteeTrackingCategories already exists. The users table already has a tracking_categories jsonb column. Build a section accessible from /mentor/classes/[classId] page (in the member list, add a 'Tracking' action per mentee) that shows current categories and lets the mentor add/edit/remove them. Each category has: key (auto-generated slug from label), label (text input), emoji (text input, default pin emoji). Save button calls setMenteeTrackingCategories. Show system defaults as placeholder when none are set.
acceptance: Mentor can view a mentee's current tracking categories
Mentor can add new categories with label and emoji
Mentor can remove categories
Changes persist to DB via setMenteeTrackingCategories
Mentee's Reflect page dynamically shows the mentor-assigned categories
npx next build succeeds

userStories.title: Admin configurable reflection questions per module
userStories.description: Currently reflection questions are hardcoded in src/app/mentee/reflection/page.tsx as DEFAULT_QUESTIONS array. Add a jsonb field 'reflection_questions' to the schwerpunktebenen table in src/lib/db/schema.ts. Add a section in the Schwerpunktebene edit dialog (src/app/admin/schwerpunktebenen/edit-schwerpunktebene-dialog.tsx) where admin can manage a list of question strings. Update the tRPC schwerpunktebenen router to accept reflection_questions on create/update. Update mentee.getCurrentWeekTheme to return reflection_questions from the monthTheme. Update the mentee reflection page to use questions from weekThemeData.monthTheme.reflectionQuestions, falling back to DEFAULT_QUESTIONS if empty/null.
acceptance: schwerpunktebenen table has reflection_questions jsonb column
Admin can add/edit/remove reflection questions in Schwerpunktebene edit dialog
Mentee reflection page shows questions from DB for current module
Falls back to hardcoded default questions when none configured
Migration SQL file exists and is applied
npx next build succeeds

userStories.title: Mentor reflection feedback UI
userStories.description: Build a page where the mentor can see submitted reflection sheets from mentees and provide written feedback. DB already has: reflection_entries table with mentor_feedback text field and mentor_feedback_at timestamp. Mentee reflection page already displays mentor feedback. Build: 1) tRPC procedure mentor.getMenteeReflections — fetches reflection_entries for a menteeId, joined with schwerpunktebenen for module names. 2) tRPC mutation mentor.saveReflectionFeedback(id, feedback) — updates mentor_feedback + mentor_feedback_at. 3) Page at /mentor/mentee/[menteeId]/reflections showing submitted reflections grouped by module with mentee answers and textarea for feedback. Import reflectionEntries and schwerpunktebenen from schema.
acceptance: Mentor can navigate to a mentee's reflections page
Page shows list of submitted reflections with module name
Each reflection shows questions and the mentee's answers
Mentor can write feedback in a textarea and save it
Saved feedback appears on the mentee's reflection page
npx next build succeeds

userStories.title: Exact program timing from class start date
userStories.description: Update the program timeline page (src/app/mentee/calendar/weekly/page.tsx) to calculate exact dates from the class start date instead of approximating from weekNumber. Add a tRPC procedure mentee.getProgramTimeline that: 1) Gets user's class via classMembers join with classes. 2) Uses classes.startDate as BASICS date. 3) Calculates: BASICS=startDate, MODULE_1=startDate+7d, MODULE_2=startDate+28d, MODULE_3=startDate+49d, MODULE_4=startDate+70d, MODULE_5=startDate+91d, END_TALK=startDate+112d. 4) Fetches booked mentoring_sessions for this user and includes their dates. 5) Returns array of {type, label, date, isBooked?}. Update the weekly page to call getProgramTimeline instead of using the approximate PROGRAM_STRUCTURE constant. Keep the existing timeline UI (vertical line, dots, module labels).
acceptance: New tRPC procedure mentee.getProgramTimeline exists and returns correct dates
Dates calculated from actual class startDate in DB
BASICS on start date, modules spaced correctly (7, 28, 49, 70, 91, 112 days offset)
Booked 1:1 sessions appear in the timeline with date
Current phase is highlighted with orange
Works when mentee has no class (shows 'no program assigned')
npx next build succeeds
